title: Build typed PR mutation lifecycle
goal: Reduce Platform-Filter friction by replacing free-form push, PR update and merge command text in the ChatGPT path with narrow receipt-bound Grabowski PR lifecycle grips or named operations.

acceptance.assertion: branch-publish is validated or hardened so it publishes only the expected local HEAD to the named branch and emits an audit receipt with blocked/pass/fail status.
pr-create-or-update is validated or hardened so PR metadata writes require expected head, base, branch and title, and never fall back to generic GitHub shell text.

A typed pr-merge grip or named operation exists and requires open non-draft PR, expected head, clean mergeability, required green checks, current review-gate PASS evidence and match-head-commit semantics.
The publish/upsert/merge lifecycle returns structured receipts that include parameters hash, observed PR/head/check state, review-gate evidence reference and explicit non-claims.

Focused tests cover expected-head mismatch, dirty checkout, missing checks, missing review evidence, stale review evidence, mergeable=false and successful happy path without invoking free-form dangerous command strings.

metadata.source: User requested phases 1-4 from the 2026-07-08 Grabowski optimization plan be registered as Bureau tasks.
metadata.live_basis: Friction ledger shows Platform-Filter as largest unresolved class; branch-publish and pr-create-or-update already exist, pr-merge as narrow normal lifecycle grip is still missing.
metadata.verification_note: Grabowski PR #152 hardened the typed branch publication and PR upsert path. PR #153 then supplied the live end-to-end proof: the exact published head was turned into a PR and merged by the provider-neutral Captain after head, diff, CI, review and repository merge-policy checks.

validated_acceptance.publish-hardened: The branch-publish receipt 770f91f5e3bcf00c965f4574f49362d5cb35f9bdf8c85c37c17fde582c0786e3 binds repository, branch and expected local head before publication.
validated_acceptance.pr-upsert-hardened: The pr-create-or-update receipt 0ae67a380c42ec66a4f5a3278479700f5498f740cbb49a2c31dd90a7dc7a590e created PR #153 on exact head e1b1f88a764168da42068d6168becbca2844e608 without generic shell fallback.
validated_acceptance.typed-pr-merge: The Captain merge receipt bc88b47cf17706e384ce645ac61a46595cb0b5c98dd5d5810f5a540ef656b1a1 records the successful policy-compatible squash merge of PR #153 after the typed evidence gates passed.
validated_acceptance.receipt-bound-lifecycle: Publish, PR creation and merge each produced a distinct receipt hash and the remote PR target state was reread after merge.
validated_acceptance.tests: PR #152 passed the full local validation suite with 1002 tests and GitHub validate (3.10)/(3.12); the follow-up proof PR #153 also passed both required checks before Captain execution.

validation.github_ci: validate (3.10) and validate (3.12) succeeded for PR #152 and PR #153 before merge
validation.live_proof: PR #153 reached MERGED at merge commit 984539139e425d44972b306e395ff87f97185e01
